units.text: Enter RICHARD, Duke of Gloucester, solus

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Now is the winter of our discontent
Made glorious summer by this son of York,
And all the clouds that loured upon our house
In the deep bosom of the ocean buried.
Now are our brows bound with victorious wreaths,
Our bruisèd arms hung up for monuments,
Our stern alarums changed to merry meetings,
Our dreadful marches to delightful measures.
Grim-visaged war hath smoothed his wrinkled front;
And now, instead of mounting barbèd steeds
To fright the souls of fearful adversaries,
He capers nimbly in a lady’s chamber
To the lascivious pleasing of a lute.
But I, that am not shaped for sportive tricks,
Nor made to court an amorous looking glass;
I, that am rudely stamped and want love’s majesty
To strut before a wanton ambling nymph;
I, that am curtailed of this fair proportion,
Cheated of feature by dissembling nature,
Deformed, unfinished, sent before my time
Into this breathing world, scarce half made up,
And that so lamely and unfashionable
That dogs bark at me as I halt by them—
Why, I, in this weak piping time of peace,
Have no delight to pass away the time,
Unless to see my shadow in the sun
And descant on mine own deformity.
And therefore, since I cannot prove a lover
To entertain these fair well-spoken days,
I am determinèd to prove a villain
And hate the idle pleasures of these days.
Plots have I laid, inductions dangerous,
By drunken prophecies, libels and dreams,
To set my brother Clarence and the king
In deadly hate, the one against the other;
And if King Edward be as true and just
As I am subtle, false, and treacherous,
This day should Clarence closely be mewed up
About a prophecy which says that “G”
Of Edward’s heirs the murderer shall be.
Dive, thoughts, down to my soul. Here Clarence comes.
Enter CLARENCE, guarded, and BRAKENBURY

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Brother, good day. What means this armèd guard
That waits upon your Grace?

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
His majesty,
Tend'ring my person’s safety, hath appointed
This conduct to convey me to the Tower.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Upon what cause?

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: Because my name is George.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Alack, my lord, that fault is none of yours.
He should, for that, commit your godfathers.
O, belike his majesty hath some intent
That you shall be new christened in the Tower.
But what’s the matter, Clarence? May I know?

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Yea, Richard, when I know, for I protest
As yet I do not. But, as I can learn,
He hearkens after prophecies and dreams,
And from the crossrow plucks the letter "G",
And says a wizard told him that by “G”
His issue disinherited should be.
And for my name of George begins with "G",
It follows in his thought that I am he.
These, as I learn, and such like toys as these
Have moved his Highness to commit me now.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Why, this it is when men are ruled by women.
'Tis not the king that sends you to the Tower.
My Lady Grey his wife, Clarence, ’tis she
That tempers him to this extremity.
Was it not she and that good man of worship,
Anthony Woodeville, her brother there,
That made him send Lord Hastings to the Tower,
From whence this present day he is delivered?
We are not safe, Clarence. We are not safe.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
By heaven, I think there is no man is secure
But the queen’s kindred and night-walking heralds
That trudge betwixt the king and Mistress Shore.
Heard ye not what an humble suppliant
Lord Hastings was to her for his delivery?

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Humbly complaining to her deity
Got my Lord Chamberlain his liberty.
I’ll tell you what: I think it is our way,
If we will keep in favor with the king,
To be her men and wear her livery.
The jealous o'erworn widow and herself,
Since that our brother dubbed them gentlewomen,
Are mighty gossips in this monarchy.

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text:
I beseech your Graces both to pardon me.
His majesty hath straitly given in charge
That no man shall have private conference,
Of what degree soever, with his brother.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Even so. An please your Worship, Brakenbury,
You may partake of anything we say.
We speak no treason, man. We say the king
Is wise and virtuous, and his noble queen
Well struck in years, fair, and not jealous.
We say that Shore’s wife hath a pretty foot,
A cherry lip, a bonny eye, a passing pleasing tongue,
And that the queen’s kindred are made gentlefolks.
How say you, sir? Can you deny all this?

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text: With this, my lord, myself have naught to do.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Naught to do with Mistress Shore? I tell thee, fellow,
He that doth naught with her, excepting one,
Were best he do it secretly, alone.

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text: What one, my lord?

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Her husband, knave. Wouldst thou betray me?

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text:
I do beseech your Grace to pardon me, and withal
Forbear your conference with the noble duke.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: We know thy charge, Brakenbury, and will obey.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
We are the queen’s abjects and must obey.—
Brother, farewell. I will unto the king,
And whatsoe'er you will employ me in,
Were it to call King Edward’s widow “sister,”
I will perform it to enfranchise you.
Meantime, this deep disgrace in brotherhood
Touches me deeper than you can imagine.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: I know it pleaseth neither of us well.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Well, your imprisonment shall not be long.
I will deliver you or else lie for you.
Meantime, have patience.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: I must perforce. Farewell.
Exeunt CLARENCE, BRAKENBURY, and guard

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Go tread the path that thou shalt ne'er return.
Simple, plain Clarence, I do love thee so
That I will shortly send thy soul to heaven,
If heaven will take the present at our hands.
But who comes here? The new-delivered Hastings?
Enter HASTINGS

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text: Good time of day unto my gracious lord.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
As much unto my good Lord Chamberlain.
Well are you welcome to the open air.
How hath your lordship brooked imprisonment?

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
With patience, noble lord, as prisoners must.
But I shall live, my lord, to give them thanks
That were the cause of my imprisonment.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
No doubt, no doubt; and so shall Clarence too,
For they that were your enemies are his
And have prevailed as much on him as you.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
More pity that the eagle should be mewed
While kites and buzzards prey at liberty.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: What news abroad?

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
No news so bad abroad as this at home:
The king is sickly, weak and melancholy,
And his physicians fear him mightily.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Now, by Saint Paul, that news is bad indeed.
O, he hath kept an evil diet long,
And overmuch consumed his royal person.
'Tis very grievous to be thought upon.
Where is he, in his bed?

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text: He is.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Go you before, and I will follow you.
Exit HASTINGS

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
He cannot live, I hope, and must not die
Till George be packed with post-horse up to heaven.
I’ll in to urge his hatred more to Clarence
With lies well steeled with weighty arguments,
And, if I fail not in my deep intent,
Clarence hath not another day to live;
Which done, God take King Edward to His mercy,
And leave the world for me to bustle in.
For then I’ll marry Warwick’s youngest daughter.
What though I killed her husband and her father?
The readiest way to make the wench amends
Is to become her husband and her father;
The which will I, not all so much for love
As for another secret close intent
By marrying her which I must reach unto.
But yet I run before my horse to market.
Clarence still breathes; Edward still lives and reigns.
When they are gone, then must I count my gains.
Exit
Enter the corse of Henry the Sixth, on a bier, with halberds to guard it, Lady ANNE being the mourner, accompanied by gentlemen

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
Set down, set down your honorable load,
If honor may be shrouded in a hearse,
Whilst I awhile obsequiously lament
Th' untimely fall of virtuous Lancaster.
Enter RICHARD, Duke of Gloucester

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Stay, you that bear the corse, and set it down.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
What black magician conjures up this fiend
To stop devoted charitable deeds?

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Villains, set down the corse or, by Saint Paul,
I’ll make a corse of him that disobeys.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: My lord, stand back and let the coffin pass.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Unmannered dog, stand thou when I command!—
Advance thy halberd higher than my breast,
Or by Saint Paul I’ll strike thee to my foot
And spurn upon thee, beggar, for thy boldness.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
[to gentlemen and halberds]
What, do you tremble? Are you all afraid?
Alas, I blame you not, for you are mortal,
And mortal eyes cannot endure the devil.—
Avaunt, thou dreadful minister of hell.
Thou hadst but power over his mortal body;
His soul thou canst not have. Therefore begone.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Sweet saint, for charity, be not so curst.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
Foul devil, for God’s sake, hence, and trouble us not,
For thou hast made the happy earth thy hell,
Filled it with cursing cries and deep exclaims.
If thou delight to view thy heinous deeds,
Behold this pattern of thy butcheries.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Lady, you know no rules of charity,
Which renders good for bad, blessings for curses.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
Villain, thou know’st not law of God nor man.
No beast so fierce but knows some touch of pity.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: But I know none, and therefore am no beast.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: O, wonderful, when devils tell the truth!

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
More wonderful, when angels are so angry.
Vouchsafe, divine perfection of a woman,
Of these supposèd crimes to give me leave
By circumstance but to acquit myself.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
Vouchsafe, defused infection of a man,
Of these known evils but to give me leave
By circumstance to curse thy cursèd self.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Fairer than tongue can name thee, let me have
Some patient leisure to excuse myself.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
Fouler than heart can think thee, thou canst make
No excuse current but to hang thyself.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: By such despair I should accuse myself.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
And by despairing shalt thou stand excused
For doing worthy vengeance on thyself
That didst unworthy slaughter upon others.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Say that I slew them not.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
Then say they were not slain.
But dead they are, and devilish slave, by thee.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: I did not kill your husband.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Why then, he is alive.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Nay, he is dead, and slain by Edward’s hands.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
In thy foul throat thou liest. Queen Margaret saw
Thy murd'rous falchion smoking in his blood,
The which thou once didst bend against her breast,
But that thy brothers beat aside the point.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
I was provokèd by her sland'rous tongue,
That laid their guilt upon my guiltless shoulders.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
Thou wast provokèd by thy bloody mind,
That never dream’st on aught but butcheries.
Didst thou not kill this king?

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: I grant you.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
Dost grant me, hedgehog? Then, God grant me too
Thou mayst be damnèd for that wicked deed.
O, he was gentle, mild, and virtuous.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: The better for the King of heaven that hath him.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: He is in heaven, where thou shalt never come.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Let him thank me, that holp to send him thither,
For he was fitter for that place than earth.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: And thou unfit for any place but hell.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Yes, one place else, if you will hear me name it.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Some dungeon.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Your bedchamber.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Ill rest betide the chamber where thou liest!

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: So will it, madam till I lie with you.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: I hope so.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
I know so. But, gentle Lady Anne,
To leave this keen encounter of our wits
And fall something into a slower method—
Is not the causer of the timeless deaths
Of these Plantagenets, Henry and Edward,
As blameful as the executioner?

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Thou wast the cause and most accursed effect.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Your beauty was the cause of that effect—
Your beauty, that did haunt me in my sleep
To undertake the death of all the world,
So I might live one hour in your sweet bosom.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
If I thought that, I tell thee, homicide,
These nails should rend that beauty from my cheeks.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
These eyes could never endure that beauty’s wrack.
You should not blemish it, if I stood by.
As all the world is cheerèd by the sun,
So I by that. It is my day, my life.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Black night o'ershade thy day, and death thy life.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Curse not thyself, fair creature; thou art both.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: I would I were, to be revenged on thee.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
It is a quarrel most unnatural
To be revenged on him that loveth thee.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
It is a quarrel just and reasonable
To be revenged on him that killed my husband.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
He that bereft thee, lady, of thy husband
Did it to help thee to a better husband.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: His better doth not breathe upon the earth.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: He lives that loves thee better than he could.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Name him.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Plantagenet.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Why, that was he.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: The selfsame name, but one of better nature.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Where is he?

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Here.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Would it were mortal poison for thy sake.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Never came poison from so sweet a place.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
Never hung poison on a fouler toad.
Out of my sight! Thou dost infect mine eyes.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Thine eyes, sweet lady, have infected mine.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Would they were basilisks to strike thee dead.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
I would they were, that I might die at once,
For now they kill me with a living death.
Those eyes of thine from mine have drawn salt tears,
Shamed their aspect with store of childish drops.
These eyes, which never shed remorseful tear—
No, when my father York and Edward wept
To hear the piteous moan that Rutland made
When black-faced Clifford shook his sword at him;
Nor when thy warlike father, like a child,
Told the sad story of my father’s death
And twenty times made pause to sob and weep,
That all the standers-by had wet their cheeks
Like trees bedashed with rain—in that sad time,
My manly eyes did scorn an humble tear;
And what these sorrows could not thence exhale
Thy beauty hath, and made them blind with weeping.
I never sued to friend, nor enemy;
My tongue could never learn sweet smoothing word.
But now thy beauty is proposed my fee,
My proud heart sues, and prompts my tongue to speak.
He kneels and lays his breast open; she offers at it with his sword

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Nay, do not pause; for I did kill King Henry—
But ’twas thy beauty that provokèd me.
Nay, now dispatch; ’twas I that stabbed young Edward—
But ’twas thy heavenly face that set me on.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
Arise, dissembler. Though I wish thy death,
I will not be the executioner.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: [rising] Then bid me kill myself, and I will do it.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: I have already.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
That was in thy rage.
Speak it again and, even with the word,
This hand, which for thy love did kill thy love,
Shall for thy love kill a far truer love.
To both their deaths shalt thou be accessory.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: I would I knew thy heart.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: 'Tis figured in my tongue.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: I fear me both are false.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Then never man was man true.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: Well, well, put up your sword.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Say then my peace is made.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: That shall you know hereafter.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: But shall I live in hope?

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: All men I hope live so.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Vouchsafe to wear this ring.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: To take is not to give.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Look, how this ring encompasseth finger;
Even so thy breast encloseth my poor heart.
Wear both of them, for both of them are thine.
And if thy poor devoted servant may
But beg one favor at thy gracious hand,
Thou dost confirm his happiness forever.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text: What is it?

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
That it would please you leave these sad designs
To him that hath more cause to be a mourner,
And presently repair to Crosby House,
Where, after I have solemnly interred
At Chertsey monast'ry this noble king
And wet his grave with my repentant tears,
I will with all expedient duty see you.
For divers unknown reasons, I beseech you,
Grant me this boon.

units.speaker: ANNE
units.text:
With all my heart, and much it joys me too
To see you are become so penitent.—
Tressel and Berkeley, go along with me.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Bid me farewell.

units.speaker: ANNE

units.text: Exeunt Lady ANNE and two others

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Sirs, take up the corse.

units.speaker: GENTLEMAN
units.text: Towards Chertsey, noble lord?

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: No, to Whitefriars. There attend my coming.
Exeunt all but RICHARD

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Was ever woman in this humor wooed?
Was ever woman in this humor won?
I’ll have her, but I will not keep her long.
What, I that killed her husband and his father,
To take her in her heart’s extremest hate,
With curses in her mouth, tears in her eyes,
The bleeding witness of my hatred by,
Having God, her conscience, and these bars against me,
And I no friends to back my suit at all
But the plain devil and dissembling looks?
And yet to win her, all the world to nothing! Ha!
Hath she forgot already that brave prince,
Edward, her lord, whom I some three months since
Stabbed in my angry mood at Tewkesbury?
A sweeter and a lovelier gentleman,
Framed in the prodigality of nature,
Young, valiant, wise, and, no doubt, right royal,
The spacious world cannot again afford.
And will she yet abase her eyes on me,
That cropped the golden prime of this sweet prince
And made her widow to a woeful bed?
On me, whose all not equals Edward’s moiety?
On me, that halts and am misshapen thus?
My dukedom to a beggarly denier,
I do mistake my person all this while!
Upon my life, she finds, although I cannot,
Myself to be a marv'lous proper man.
I’ll be at charges for a looking glass
And entertain a score or two of tailors
To study fashions to adorn my body.
Since I am crept in favor with myself,
I will maintain it with some little cost.
But first I’ll turn yon fellow in his grave
And then return lamenting to my love.
Shine out, fair sun, till I have bought a glass,
That I may see my shadow as I pass.
Exit
Enter QUEEN ELIZABETH, Lord Marquess of DORSET, Lord RIVERS, and Lord GREY

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text:
Have patience, madam. There’s no doubt his majesty
Will soon recover his accustomed health.

units.speaker: GREY
units.text:
In that you brook it ill, it makes him worse.
Therefore, for God’s sake, entertain good comfort
And cheer his grace with quick and merry eyes.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: If he were dead, what would betide on me?

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: No other harm but loss of such a lord.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: The loss of such a lord includes all harms.

units.speaker: GREY
units.text:
The heavens have blessed you with a goodly son
To be your comforter when he is gone.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
Ah, he is young, and his minority
Is put unto the trust of Richard Gloucester,
A man that loves not me nor none of you.

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: Is it concluded that he shall be Protector?

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
It is determined, not concluded yet;
But so it must be if the king miscarry.
Enter BUCKINGHAM and Lord STANLEY, Earl of Derby

units.speaker: GREY
units.text: Here comes the lord of Buckingham, and Derby.

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text:
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH] Good time of day unto your royal
Grace.

units.speaker: STANLEY
units.text: God make your Majesty joyful, as you have been.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
The countess Richmond, good my lord of Derby,
To your good prayer will scarcely say amen.
Yet, Derby, notwithstanding she’s your wife
And loves not me, be you, good lord, assured
I hate not you for her proud arrogance.

units.speaker: STANLEY
units.text:
I do beseech you either not believe
The envious slanders of her false accusers,
Or if she be accused in true report,
Bear with her weakness, which I think proceeds
From wayward sickness and no grounded malice.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: Saw you the king today, my lord of Derby?

units.speaker: STANLEY
units.text:
But now the duke of Buckingham and I
Are come from visiting his majesty.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: What likelihood of his amendment, lords?

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text: Madam, good hope. His grace speaks cheerfully.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: God grant him health. Did you confer with him?

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text:
Ay, madam. He desires to make atonement
Betwixt the duke of Gloucester and your brothers,
And betwixt them and my Lord Chamberlain,
And sent to warn them to his royal presence.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
Would all were well—but that will never be.
I fear our happiness is at the height.
Enter RICHARD, Duke of Gloucester, and HASTINGS

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
They do me wrong, and I will not endure it!
Who is it that complains unto the king
That I, forsooth, am stern and love them not?
By holy Paul, they love his grace but lightly
That fill his ears with such dissentious rumors.
Because I cannot flatter and look fair,
Smile in men’s faces, smooth, deceive and cog,
Duck with French nods and apish courtesy,
I must be held a rancorous enemy.
Cannot a plain man live and think no harm,
But thus his simple truth must be abused
With silken, sly, insinuating jacks?

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: To whom in all this presence speaks your Grace?

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
To thee, that hast nor honesty nor grace.
When have I injured thee? When done thee wrong?—
Or thee?—Or thee? Or any of your faction?
A plague upon you all! His royal grace,
Whom God preserve better than you would wish,
Cannot be quiet scarce a breathing while
But you must trouble him with lewd complaints.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
Brother of Gloucester, you mistake the matter.
The king, on his own royal disposition,
And not provoked by any suitor else,
Aiming belike at your interior hatred
That in your outward actions shows itself
Against my children, brothers, and myself,
Makes him to send, that he may learn the ground
Of your ill will, and thereby to remove it.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
I cannot tell. The world is grown so bad
That wrens make prey where eagles dare not perch.
Since every jack became a gentleman,
There’s many a gentle person made a jack.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
Come, come, we know your meaning, brother Gloucester.
You envy my advancement, and my friends'.
God grant we never may have need of you.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Meantime God grants that we have need of you.
Our brother is imprisoned by your means,
Myself disgraced, and the nobility
Held in contempt, while great promotions
Are daily given to ennoble those
That scarce some two days since were worth a noble.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
By Him that raised me to this careful height
From that contented hap which I enjoyed,
I never did incense his majesty
Against the duke of Clarence, but have been
An earnest advocate to plead for him.
My lord, you do me shameful injury
Falsely to draw me in these vile suspects.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
You may deny that you were not the mean
Of my Lord Hastings' late imprisonment.

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: She may, my lord, for—

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
She may, Lord Rivers. Why, who knows not so?
She may do more, sir, than denying that.
She may help you to many fair preferments
And then deny her aiding hand therein,
And lay those honors on your high desert.
What may she not? She may, ay, marry, may she—

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: What, marry, may she?

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
What, marry, may she? Marry with a king,
A bachelor, a handsome stripling too.
I wis, your grandam had a worser match.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
My Lord of Gloucester, I have too long borne
Your blunt upbraidings and your bitter scoffs.
By heaven, I will acquaint his majesty
With those gross taunts that oft I have endured.
I had rather be a country servant-maid
Than a great queen with this condition,
To be so baited, scorned, and stormèd at.
Enter old QUEEN MARGARET, apart from others

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: Small joy have I in being England’s queen.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
[aside] And lessened be that small, God I beseech Him!
Thy honor, state, and seat is due to me.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH] What, threat you me with telling
of the king?
Tell him, and spare not. Look, what I have said,
I will avouch ’t in presence of the king;
I dare adventure to be sent to th' Tower.
'Tis time to speak. My pains are quite forgot.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
[aside] Out, devil! I do remember them too well:
Thou killed’st my husband Henry in the Tower,
And Edward, my poor son, at Tewkesbury.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH] Ere you were queen, ay, or your
husband king,
I was a packhorse in his great affairs,
A weeder-out of his proud adversaries,
A liberal rewarder of his friends.
To royalize his blood, I spent mine own.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text: [aside] Ay, and much better blood than his or thine.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH] In all which time, you and your
husband Grey
Were factious for the house of Lancaster.—
And, Rivers, so were you.— Was not your husband
In Margaret’s battle at Saint Albans slain?
Let me put in your minds, if you forget,
What you have been ere this, and what you are;
Withal, what I have been, and what I am.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text: [aside] A murd'rous villain, and so still thou art.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH] Poor Clarence did forsake his
father Warwick,
Ay, and forswore himself—which Jesu pardon!—

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text: [aside] Which God revenge!

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
To fight on Edward’s party for the crown;
And for his meed, poor lord, he is mewed up.
I would to God my heart were flint, like Edward’s,
Or Edward’s soft and pitiful, like mine.
I am too childish-foolish for this world.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
[aside] Hie thee to hell for shame, and leave the
world,
Thou cacodemon! There thy kingdom is.

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text:
My Lord of Gloucester, in those busy days
Which here you urge to prove us enemies,
We followed then our lord, our sovereign king.
So should we you, if you should be our king.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
If I should be? I had rather be a peddler.
Far be it from my heart, the thought thereof.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
As little joy, my lord, as you suppose
You should enjoy were you this country’s king,
As little joy may you suppose in me
That I enjoy, being the queen thereof.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
[aside] Ah, little joy enjoys the queen thereof,
For I am she, and altogether joyless.
I can no longer hold me patient.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Foul, wrinkled witch, what mak’st thou in my sight?

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
But repetition of what thou hast marred.
That will I make before I let thee go.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Wert thou not banishèd on pain of death?

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
I was, but I do find more pain in banishment
Than death can yield me here by my abode.
A husband and a son thou ow’st to me;
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH]
And thou a kingdom; — all of you, allegiance.
The sorrow that I have by right is yours,
And all the pleasures you usurp are mine.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
The curse my noble father laid on thee
When thou didst crown his warlike brows with paper,
And with thy scorns drew’st rivers from his eyes,
And then, to dry them, gav’st the duke a clout
Steeped in the faultless blood of pretty Rutland—
His curses then, from bitterness of soul
Denounced against thee, are all fall'n upon thee,
And God, not we, hath plagued thy bloody deed.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: So just is God to right the innocent.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
O, ’twas the foulest deed to slay that babe,
And the most merciless that e'er was heard of!

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: Tyrants themselves wept when it was reported.

units.speaker: DORSET
units.text: No man but prophesied revenge for it.

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text: Northumberland, then present, wept to see it.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
What, were you snarling all before I came,
Ready to catch each other by the throat,
And turn you all your hatred now on me?
Did York’s dread curse prevail so much with heaven
That Henry’s death, my Lovelly Edward’s death,
Their kingdom’s loss, my woeful banishment,
Could all but answer for that peevish brat?
Can curses pierce the clouds and enter heaven?
Why then, give way, dull clouds, to my quick curses!
Though not by war, by surfeit die your king,
As ours by murder to make him a king.
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH]
Edward thy son, that now is Prince of Wales,
For Edward our son, that was Prince of Wales,
Die in his youth by like untimely violence.
Thyself a queen, for me that was a queen,
Outlive they glory, like my wretched self.
Long mayst thou live to wail they children's death
And see another, as I see thee now,
Decked in they rights, as thou art stalled in mine.
Long die thy happy days before they death,
And, after many lengthened hours of grief,
Die neither mother, wife, nor England's queen.
Rivers and Dorset, you were standers-by,
And so wast thou, Lord Hastings, when my son
Was stabbed with bloody daggers. God I pray Him
That none of you may live his natural age,
But by some unlooked accident cut off.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Have done thy charm, thou hateful, withered hag.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
And leave out thee? Stay, dog, for thou shalt hear me.
If heaven have any grievous plague in store
Exceeding those that I can wish upon thee,
O, let them keep it till thy sins be ripe
And then hurl down their indignation
On thee, the troubler of the poor world’s peace.
The worm of conscience still begnaw thy soul.
Thy friends suspect for traitors while thou liv’st,
And take deep traitors for thy dearest friends.
No sleep close up that deadly eye of thine,
Unless it be while some tormenting dream
Affrights thee with a hell of ugly devils.
Thou elvish-marked, abortive, rooting hog,
Thou was sealed in they nativity
The slave of nature and the son of hell,
Thou slander of they heavy mother's womb,
Thou loathed issue of they father's loins,
Thou rag of honor, thou detested—

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Margaret.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text: Richard!

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Ha?

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text: I call thee not.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
I cry thee mercy, then, for I did think
That thou hadst called me all these bitter names.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
Why, so I did, but looked for no reply.
O, let me make the period to my curse!

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: 'Tis done by me, and ends in “Margaret.”

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
[to QUEEN MARGARET]
Thus have you breathed your curse against yourself.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
Poor painted queen, vain flourish of my fortune,
Why strew’st thou sugar on that bottled spider,
Whose deadly web ensnareth thee about?
Fool, fool, thou whet’st a knife to kill thyself.
The day will come that thou shalt wish for me
To help thee curse that poisonous bunch-backed toad.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text:
False-boding woman, end thy frantic curse,
Lest to thy harm thou move our patience.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text: Foul shame upon you, you have all moved mine.

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: Were you well served, you would be taught your duty.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
To serve me well, you all should do me duty:
Teach me to be your queen, and you my subjects.
O, serve me well, and teach yourselves that duty!

units.speaker: DORSET
units.text: [to RIVERS] Dispute not with her; she is lunatic.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
Peace, Master Marquess, you are malapert.
Your fire-new stamp of honor is scarce current.
O, that your young nobility could judge
What ’twere to lose it and be miserable!
They that stand high have many blasts to shake them,
And if they fall, they dash themselves to pieces.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: Good counsel, marry. —Learn it, learn it, marquess.

units.speaker: DORSET
units.text: It touches you, my lord, as much as me.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Ay, and much more; but I was born so high.
Our aerie buildeth in the cedar’s top,
And dallies with the wind and scorns the sun.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
And turns the sun to shade. Alas, alas,
Witness my son, now in the shade of death,
Whose bright out-shining beams thy cloudy wrath
Hath in eternal darkness folded up.
Your aerie buildeth in our aerie’s nest.
O God, that seest it, do not suffer it!
As it was won with blood, lost be it so.

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text: Peace, peace, for shame, if not for charity.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
Urge neither charity nor shame to me.
[addressing the others]
Uncharitably with me have you dealt,
And shamefully my hopes by you are butchered.
My charity is outrage, life my shame,
And in that shame still live my sorrows' rage.

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text: Have done, have done.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
O princely Buckingham, I’ll kiss thy hand
In sign of league and amity with thee.
Now fair befall thee and thy noble house!
Thy garments are not spotted with our blood,
Nor thou within the compass of my curse.

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text:
Nor no one here, for curses never pass
The lips of those that breathe them in the air.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
I will not think but they ascend the sky,
And there awake God’s gentle-sleeping peace.
[aside to BUCKINGHAM]
O Buckingham, take heed of yonder dog!
Look when he fawns, he bites; and when he bites,
His venom tooth will rankle to the death.
Have naught to do with him. Beware of him.
Sin, death, and hell have set their marks on him,
And all their ministers attend on him.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: What doth she say, my lord of Buckingham?

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text: Nothing that I respect, my gracious lord.

units.speaker: QUEEN MARGARET
units.text:
What, dost thou scorn me for my gentle counsel,
And soothe the devil that I warn thee from?
O, but remember this another day,
When he shall split thy very heart with sorrow,
And say poor Margaret was a prophetess.—
Live each of you the subjects to his hate,
And he to yours, and all of you to God’s.
Exit

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text: My hair doth stand an end to hear her curses.

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: And so doth mine. I muse why she’s at liberty.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
I cannot blame her. By God’s holy mother,
She hath had too much wrong, and I repent
My part thereof that I have done to her.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: I never did her any, to my knowledge.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Yet you have all the vantage of her wrong.
I was too hot to do somebody good
That is too cold in thinking of it now.
Marry, as for Clarence, he is well repaid;
He is franked up to fatting for his pains.
God pardon them that are the cause thereof.

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text:
A virtuous and a Christian-like conclusion
To pray for them that have done scathe to us.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
So do I ever [aside] being well-advised,
For had I cursed now, I had cursed myself.
Enter CATESBY

units.speaker: CATESBY
units.text:
Madam, his majesty doth call for you,—
And for your Grace, —and yours, my gracious lords.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: Catesby, I come. —Lords, will you go with me?

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: We wait upon your Grace.
Exeunt all but RICHARD, Duke of Gloucester

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
I do the wrong, and first begin to brawl.
The secret mischiefs that I set abroach
I lay unto the grievous charge of others.
Clarence, whom I indeed have cast in darkness,
I do beweep to many simple gulls,
Namely, to Derby, Hastings, Buckingham,
And tell them ’tis the queen and her allies
That stir the king against the duke my brother.
Now they believe it and withal whet me
To be revenged on Rivers, Dorset, Grey;
But then I sigh and, with a piece of scripture,
Tell them that God bids us do good for evil;
And thus I clothe my naked villainy
With odd old ends stolen out of Holy Writ,
And seem a saint when most I play the devil.
Enter two MURDERERS

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
But, soft! here come my executioners.—
How now, my hardy, stout, resolvèd mates?
Are you now going to dispatch this thing?

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
We are, my lord, and come to have the warrant
That we may be admitted where he is.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Well thought upon. I have it here about me.
[He gives a paper]
When you have done, repair to Crosby Place.
But, sirs, be sudden in the execution,
Withal obdurate; do not hear him plead,
For Clarence is well-spoken and perhaps
May move your hearts to pity if you mark him.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
Tut, tut, my lord, we will not stand to prate.
Talkers are no good doers. Be assured
We go to use our hands and not our tongues.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Your eyes drop millstones, when fools' eyes drop tears.
I like you lads. About your business straight.
Go, go, dispatch.

units.speaker: MURDERERS
units.text: We will, my noble lord.
Exeunt
Enter CLARENCE and BRAKENBURY

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text: Why looks your grace so heavily today?

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
O, I have passed a miserable night,
So full of ugly dreams, of ugly sights,
That, as I am a Christian faithful man,
I would not spend another such a night
Though ’twere to buy a world of happy days,
So full of dismal terror was the time.

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text: What was your dream, my lord? I pray you tell me.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Methoughts that I had broken from the Tower
And was embarked to cross to Burgundy,
And in my company my brother Gloucester,
Who from my cabin tempted me to walk
Upon the hatches. Thence we looked toward England
And cited up a thousand fearful times,
During the wars of York and Lancaster
That had befall'n us. As we paced along
Upon the giddy footing of the hatches,
Methought that Gloucester stumbled, and in falling
Struck me, that thought to stay him, overboard
Into the tumbling billows of the main.
O Lord, methought what pain it was to drown,
What dreadful noise of waters in my ears,
What sights of ugly death within my eyes.
Methoughts I saw a thousand fearful wracks,
Ten thousand men that fishes gnawed upon,
Wedges of gold, great anchors, heaps of pearl,
Inestimable stones, unvalued jewels,
All scattered in the bottom of the sea.
Some lay in dead men's skulls, and in the holes
Where eyes did once inhabit, there were crept—
as 'twere in scorn of eyes— reflecting gems,
That wooed the slimy bottom of the deep
And mocked the dead bones that lay scattered by.

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text:
Had you such leisure in the time of death
To gaze upon the secrets of the deep?

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Methought I had, and often did I strive
To yield the ghost, but still the envious flood
Stopped in my soul and would not let it forth
To find the empty, vast, and wand'ring air,
But smothered it within my panting bulk,
Who almost burst to belch it in the sea.

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text: Awaked you not in this sore agony?

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
No, no, my dream was lengthened after life.
O, then began the tempest to my soul.
I passed, methought, the melancholy flood,
With that sour ferryman which poets write of,
Unto the kingdom of perpetual night.
The first that there did greet my stranger-soul
Was my great father-in-law, renownèd Warwick,
Who cried aloud, “What scourge for perjury
Can this dark monarchy afford false Clarence?”
And so he vanished. Then came wand'ring by
A shadow like an angel, with bright hair
Dabbled in blood, and he shrieked out aloud
"Clarence is come—false, fleeting, perjured Clarence,
That stabbed me in the field by Tewkesbury.
Seize on him, furies. Take him unto torment."
With that, methoughts, a legion of foul fiends
Environed me and howled in mine ears
Such hideous cries that with the very noise
I trembling waked, and for a season after
Could not believe but that I was in hell,
Such terrible impression made my dream.

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text:
No marvel, lord, though it affrighted you.
I am afraid, methinks, to hear you tell it.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Ah keeper, keeper, I have done those things,
That now give evidence against my soul,
For Edward’s sake, and see how he requites me.—
O God, if my deep prayers cannot appease thee,
But thou wilt be avenged on my misdeeds,
Yet execute thy wrath in me alone!
O, spare my guiltless wife and my poor children!—
Keeper, I prithee sit by me awhile.
My soul is heavy, and I fain would sleep.

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text: I will, my lord. God give your Grace good rest.
CLARENCE sleeps

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text:
Sorrow breaks seasons and reposing hours,
Makes the night morning, and the noontide night.
Princes have but their titles for their glories,
An outward honor for an inward toil,
And, for unfelt imaginations,
They often feel a world of restless cares,
So that betwixt their titles and low name
There's nothing differs but the outward fame.
Enter the two MURDERERS

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Ho, who’s here?

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text: What wouldst thou, fellow? And how cam’st thou hither?

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
I would speak with Clarence, and I came hither on my
legs.

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text: What, so brief?

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
'Tis better, sir, to be brief than tedious.—Let him see
our commission, and talk no more.

units.speaker: BRAKENBURY
units.text:
I am in this commanded to deliver
The noble duke of Clarence to your hands.
I will not reason what is meant hereby
Because I will be guiltless from the meaning.
There lies the duke asleep, and there the keys.
[He hands them the keys]
I’ll to the king and signify to him
That thus I have resigned my charge to you.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: You may, sir. 'Tis a point of wisdom. Fare you well.
Exit BRAKENBURY
What, shall I stab him as he sleeps?

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: No. He’ll say ’twas done cowardly, when he wakes.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: Why, he shall never wake until the great Judgment Day.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Why, then he’ll say we stabbed him sleeping.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
The urging of that word “judgment” hath bred a kind of
remorse in me.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: What, art thou afraid?

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
Not to kill him, having a warrant, but to be damned
for killing him, from the which no warrant can defend
me.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: I thought thou hadst been resolute.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: So I am—to let him live.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: I’ll back to the duke of Gloucester and tell him so.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
Nay, I prithee stay a little. I hope this passionate
humor of mine will change. It was wont to hold me but
while one tells twenty.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: How dost thou feel thyself now?

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
Faith, some certain dregs of conscience are yet within
me.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Remember our reward when the deed’s done.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: Zounds, he dies! I had forgot the reward.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Where’s thy conscience now?

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: O, in the duke of Gloucester’s purse.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
So when he opens his purse to give us our reward, thy
conscience flies out.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
'Tis no matter. Let it go. There’s few or none will
entertain it.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: What if it come to thee again?

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
I’ll not meddle with it. It makes a man a coward: a man
cannot steal but it accuseth him; a man cannot swear
but it checks him; a man cannot lie with his neighbor’s
wife but it detects him. 'Tis a blushing, shamefaced
spirit that mutinies in a man’s bosom. It fills a man
full of obstacles. It made me once restore a purse of
gold that by chance I found. It beggars any man that
keeps it. It is turned out of towns and cities for a
dangerous thing, and every man that means to live well
endeavors to trust to himself and live without it.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
Zounds, ’tis even now at my elbow, persuading me not to
kill the duke.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
Take the devil in thy mind, and believe him not. He
would insinuate with thee but to make thee sigh.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: I am strong-framed. He cannot prevail with me.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
Spoke like a tall man that respects thy reputation.
Come, shall we fall to work?

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
Take him on the costard with the hilts of thy sword,
and then throw him into the malmsey butt in the next
room.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: O excellent device— and make a sop of him.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Soft, he wakes.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: Strike!

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: No, we’ll reason with him.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: Where art thou, keeper? Give me a cup of wine.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: You shall have wine enough, my lord, anon.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: In God’s name, what art thou?

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: A man, as you are.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: But not, as I am, royal.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Nor you, as we are, loyal.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: Thy voice is thunder, but thy looks are humble.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: My voice is now the king’s, my looks mine own.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
How darkly and how deadly dost thou speak!
Your eyes do menace me. Why look you pale?
Who sent you hither? Wherefore do you come?

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: To, to, to—

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: To murder me?

units.speaker: BOTH MURDERERS
units.text: Ay, ay.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
You scarcely have the hearts to tell me so,
And therefore cannot have the hearts to do it.
Wherein, my friends, have I offended you?

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Offended us you have not, but the king.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: I shall be reconciled to him again.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: Never, my lord. Therefore prepare to die.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Are you drawn forth among a world of men
To slay the innocent? What is my offense?
Where is the evidence that doth accuse me?
What lawful quest have given their verdict up
Unto the frowning judge? Or who pronounced
The bitter sentence of poor Clarence' death
Before I be convict by course of law?
To threaten me with death is most unlawful.
I charge you, as you hope to have redemption,
By Christ’s dear blood shed for our grievous sins,
That you depart, and lay no hands on me.
The deed you undertake is damnable.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: What we will do, we do upon command.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: And he that hath commanded is our king.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Erroneous vassals, the great King of kings
Hath in the tables of His law commanded
That thou shalt do no murder. Will thou then
Spurn at His edict and fulfill a man’s?
Take heed, for He holds vengeance in His hand
To hurl upon their heads that break His law.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
And that same vengeance doth He hurl on thee
For false forswearing and for murder too.
Thou didst receive the sacrament to fight
In quarrel of the house of Lancaster.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
And, like a traitor to the name of God,
Didst break that vow, and with thy treacherous blade
Unrippedst the bowels of thy sovereign’s son.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: Whom thou wert sworn to cherish and defend.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
How canst thou urge God’s dreadful law to us
When thou hast broke it in such dear degree?

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Alas! For whose sake did I that ill deed?
For Edward, for my brother, for his sake.
He sends you not to murder me for this,
For in that sin he is as deep as I.
If God will be avengèd for this deed,
O, know you yet He doth it publicly!
Take not the quarrel from His powerful arm;
He needs no indirect or lawless course
To cut off those that have offended Him.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
Who made thee then a bloody minister
When gallant-springing, brave Plantagenet,
That princely novice, was struck dead by thee?

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: My brother’s love, the devil, and my rage.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
Thy brother’s love, our duty, and thy faults
Provoke us hither now to slaughter thee.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
If you do love my brother, hate not me.
I am his brother, and I love him well.
If you are hired for meed, go back again,
And I will send you to my brother Gloucester,
Who shall reward you better for my life
Than Edward will for tidings of my death.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: You are deceived. Your brother Gloucester hates you.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
O, no, he loves me, and he holds me dear.
Go you to him from me.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Ay, so we will.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Tell him, when that our princely father York
Blessed his three sons with his victorious arm,
And charged us from his soul to love each other,
He little thought of this divided friendship.
Bid Gloucester think of this, and he will weep.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Ay, millstones, as he lessoned us to weep.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text: O, do not slander him, for he is kind.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
As snow in harvest. Come, you deceive yourself.
'Tis he that sends us to destroy you here.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
It cannot be, for he bewept my fortune,
And hugged me in his arms, and swore with sobs
That he would labor my delivery.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
Why, so he doth, when he delivers you
From this earth’s thralldom to the joys of heaven.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Make peace with God, for you must die, my lord.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Have you that holy feeling in your souls
To counsel me to make my peace with God,
And art you yet to your own souls so blind
That thou will war with God by murd'ring me?
O sirs, consider: they that set you on
To do this deed will hate you for the deed.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: [to FIRST MURDERER] What shall we do?

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Relent, and save your souls.
Which of you—if you were a prince's son
Being pent from liberty, as I am now—
If two such murderers as yourselves came to you,
Would you not entreat for life? Ay, you would beg,
Were you in my distress.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text: Relent? No. 'Tis cowardly and womanish.

units.speaker: CLARENCE
units.text:
Not to relent is beastly, savage, devilish.
[to SECOND MURDERER]
My friend, I spy some pity in thy looks.
O, if thine eye be not a flatterer,
Come thou on my side and entreat for me.
A begging prince what beggar pities not?

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text: Look behind you, my lord.

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
Take that, and that. [stabs CLARENCE]
If all this will not do,
I’ll drown you in the malmsey butt within.
Exit with the body

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
A bloody deed, and desperately dispatched.
How fain, like Pilate, would I wash my hands
Of this most grievous murder.
Enter FIRST MURDERER

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
How now? What mean’st thou, that thou help’st me not?
By heavens, the duke shall know how slack you have
been.

units.speaker: SECOND MURDERER
units.text:
I would he knew that I had saved his brother.
Take thou the fee, and tell him what I say,
For I repent me that the duke is slain.
Exit

units.speaker: FIRST MURDERER
units.text:
So do not I. Go, coward as thou art.
Well, I’ll go hide the body in some hole
Till that the duke give order for his burial.
And when I have my meed, I will away,
For this will out, and then I must not stay.
Exit
Flourish. Enter KING EDWARD IV, sick, QUEEN ELIZABETH, Lord Marquess DORSET, RIVERS, HASTINGS, BUCKINGHAM, GREY, and others

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text:
Why, so. Now have I done a good day’s work.
You peers, continue this united league.
I every day expect an embassage
From my Redeemer to redeem me hence,
And more in peace my soul shall part to heaven
Since I have made my friends at peace on earth
Rivers and Hastings, take each other’s hand.
Dissemble not your hatred. Swear your love.

units.speaker: RIVERS
HASTINGS
units.text: So thrive I as I truly swear the like.

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text:
Take heed you dally not before your king,
Lest He that is the supreme King of kings
Confound your hidden falsehood, and award
Either of you to be the other’s end.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text: So prosper I as I swear perfect love.

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: And I as I love Hastings with my heart.

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text:
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH]
Madam, yourself is not exempt in this,—
Nor you, son Dorset, —Buckingham, nor you.
You have been factious one against the other.—
Wife, love Lord Hastings. Let him kiss your hand,
And what you do, do it unfeignedly.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
There, Hastings, I will never more remember
Our former hatred, so thrive I and mine.
HASTINGS kisses her hand

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text: Dorset, embrace him.—Hastings, love Lord Marquess.

units.speaker: DORSET
units.text:
This interchange of love, I here protest,
Upon my part shall be inviolable.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text: And so swear I.
They embrace

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text:
Now, princely Buckingham, seal thou this league
With thy embracements to my wife’s allies
And make me happy in your unity.

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text:
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH]
Whenever Buckingham doth turn his hate
Upon your Grace, but with all duteous love
Doth cherish you and yours, God punish me
With hate in those where I expect most love.
When I have most need to employ a friend,
And most assurèd that he is a friend,
Deep, hollow, treacherous, and full of guile
Be he unto me: this do I beg of God
When I am cold in love to you or yours.

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text:
A pleasing cordial, princely Buckingham,
Is this thy vow unto my sickly heart.
There wanteth now our brother Gloucester here
To make the blessèd period of this peace.

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text:
And in good time,
Here comes Sir Richard Ratcliffe and the duke.
Enter RICHARD and RATCLIFFE

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Good morrow to my sovereign king and queen,
And, princely peers, a happy time of day.

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text:
Happy indeed, as we have spent the day.
Brother, we have done deeds of charity,
Made peace of enmity, fair love of hate,
Between these swelling, wrong-incensèd peers.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
A blessèd labor, my most sovereign lord.
Amongst this princely heap, if any here
By false intelligence, or wrong surmise
Hold me a foe,
If I unwittingly, or in my rage,
Have aught committed that is hardly borne
By any in this presence, I desire
To reconcile me to his friendly peace.
'Tis death to me to be at enmity;
I hate it, and desire all good men’s love.
First, madam, I entreat true peace of you,
Which I will purchase with my duteous service;—
Of you, my noble cousin Buckingham,
If ever any grudge were lodged between us;—
Of you, Lord Rivers, and Lord Gray of you,
Dukes, earls, lords, gentlemen; indeed of all!
I do not know that Englishman alive
With whom my soul is any jot at odds
More than the infant that is born tonight.
I thank my God for my humility.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
A holy day shall this be kept hereafter.
I would to God all strifes were well compounded.
My sovereign lord, I do beseech your Highness
To take our brother Clarence to your grace.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Why, madam, have I offered love for this,
To be so flouted in this royal presence?
Who knows not that the gentle duke is dead?

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text: Who knows not he is dead! Who knows he is?

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: All-seeing heaven, what a world is this!

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text: Look I so pale, Lord Dorset, as the rest?

units.speaker: DORSET
units.text:
Ay, my good lord, and no one in the presence
But his red color hath forsook his cheeks.

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text: Is Clarence dead? The order was reversed.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
But he, poor man, by your first order died,
And that a wingèd Mercury did bear.
Some tardy cripple bear the countermand,
That came too lag to see him burièd.
God grant that some, less noble and less loyal,
Nearer in bloody thoughts, and not in blood,
Deserve not worse than wretched Clarence did,
And yet go current from suspicion.
Enter Lord STANLEY, Earl of Derby

units.speaker: STANLEY
units.text: [kneeling] A boon, my sovereign, for my service done.

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text: I prithee, peace. My soul is full of sorrow.

units.speaker: STANLEY
units.text: I will not rise unless your Highness hear me.

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text: Then say at once what is it thou requests.

units.speaker: STANLEY
units.text:
The forfeit, sovereign, of my servant’s life,
Who slew today a riotous gentleman
Lately attendant on the duke of Norfolk.

units.speaker: KING EDWARD
units.text:
Have I a tongue to doom my brother’s death,
And shall the tongue give pardon to a slave?
My brother killed no man; his fault was thought,
And yet his punishment was bitter death.
Who sued to me for him? Who, in my wrath,
Kneeled at my feet, and bade me be advised?
Who spoke of brotherhood? Who spoke of love?
Who told me how the poor soul did forsake
The mighty Warwick and did fight for me?
Who told me, in the field by Tewkesbury,
When Oxford had me down, he rescued me,
And said “Dear brother, live, and be a king?”
Who told me, when we both lay in the field
Frozen almost to death, how he did lap me
Even in his garments and did give himself,
All thin and naked, to the numb-cold night?
All this from my remembrance brutish wrath
Sinfully plucked, and not a man of you
Had so much grace to put it in my mind.
But when your carters or your waiting vassals
Have done a drunken slaughter and defaced
The precious image of our dear Redeemer,
You straight are on your knees for pardon, pardon,
And I, unjustly too, must grant it you.
[STANLEY rises]
But for my brother, not a man would speak,
Nor I, ungracious, speak unto myself
For him, poor soul. The proudest of you all
Have been beholding to him in his life,
Yet none of you would once beg for his life.
O God, I fear Thy justice will take hold
On me and you, and mine and yours for this!—
Come, Hastings, help me to my closet.—
Ah, poor Clarence.
Exeunt some with KING EDWARD IV and QUEEN ELIZABETH

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
This is the fruits of rashness. Marked you not
How that the guilty kindred of the queen
Looked pale when they did hear of Clarence' death?
O, they did urge it still unto the king.
God will revenge it. Come, lords, will you go
To comfort Edward with our company?

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text: We wait upon your Grace.
Exeunt
Enter the old DUCHESS of York, with the two children of Clarence

units.speaker: BOY
units.text: Good grandam, tell us, is our father dead?

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: No, boy.

units.speaker: GIRL
units.text:
Why do you weep so oft, and beat your breast,
And cry, “O Clarence, my unhappy son?”

units.speaker: BOY
units.text:
Why do you look on us and shake your head,
And call us orphans, wretches, castaways,
If that our noble father were alive?

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text:
My pretty cousins, you mistake me both.
I do lament the sickness of the king,
As loath to lose him, not your father’s death.
It were lost sorrow to wail one that’s lost.

units.speaker: BOY
units.text:
Then, you conclude, my grandam, he is dead.
The king mine uncle is to blame for it.
God will revenge it, whom I will importune
With earnest prayers, all to that effect.

units.speaker: GIRL
units.text: And so will I.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text:
Peace, children, peace. The king doth love you well.
Incapable and shallow innocents,
You cannot guess who caused your father’s death.

units.speaker: BOY
units.text:
Grandam, we can, for my good uncle Gloucester
Told me the king, provoked to it by the queen,
Devised impeachments to imprison him;
And when my uncle told me so, he wept,
And pitied me, and kindly kissed my cheek,
Bade me rely on him as on my father,
And he would love me dearly as a child.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text:
Ah, that deceit should steal such gentle shape,
And with a virtuous visor hide deep vice.
He is my son, ay, and therein my shame,
Yet from my dugs he drew not this deceit.

units.speaker: BOY
units.text: Think you my uncle did dissemble, grandam?

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: Ay, boy.

units.speaker: BOY
units.text: I cannot think it. Hark, what noise is this?
Enter QUEEN ELIZABETH, with her hair about her ears, RIVERS, and DORSET after her

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
Ah, who shall hinder me to wail and weep,
To chide my fortune and torment myself?
I’ll join with black despair against my soul
And to myself become an enemy.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: What means this scene of rude impatience?

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
To make an act of tragic violence.
Edward, my lord, thy son, our king, is dead.
Why grow the branches when the root is gone?
Why wither not the leaves that want their sap?
If you will live, lament. If die, be brief,
That our swift-wingèd souls may catch the king’s,
Or, like obedient subjects, follow him
To his new kingdom of ne'er-changing night.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text:
Ah, so much interest have I in thy sorrow
As I had title in thy noble husband.
I have bewept a worthy husband’s death
And lived with looking on his images;
But now two mirrors of his princely semblance
Are cracked in pieces by malignant death,
And I, for comfort, have but one false glass
That grieves me when I see my shame in him.
Thou art a widow, yet thou art a mother,
And hast the comfort of thy children left,
But death hath snatched my husband from mine arms
And plucked two crutches from my feeble hands,
Clarence and Edward. O, what cause have I,
Thine being but a moiety of my moan,
To overgo thy woes and drown thy cries!

units.speaker: BOY
units.text:
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH] Ah, aunt, you wept not for
our father’s death.
How can we aid you with our kindred tears?

units.speaker: GIRL
units.text:
Our fatherless distress was left unmoaned.
Your widow-dolor likewise be unwept!

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
G ive me no help in lamentation.
I am not barren to bring forth complaints.
All springs reduce their currents to mine eyes,
That I, being governed by the watery moon,
May send forth plenteous tears to drown the world.
Ah, for my husband, for my dear lord Edward!

units.speaker: CHILDREN
units.text: Ah, for our father, for our dear lord Clarence!

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: Alas for both, both mine, Edward and Clarence!

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: What stay had I but Edward? And he’s gone.

units.speaker: CHILDREN
units.text: What stay had we but Clarence? And he’s gone.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: What stays had I but they? And they are gone.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: Was never widow had so dear a loss.

units.speaker: CHILDREN
units.text: Were never orphans had so dear a loss.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text:
Was never mother had so dear a loss.
Alas, I am the mother of these griefs.
Their woes are parceled; mine are general.
She for an Edward weeps, and so do I;
I for a Clarence weep; so doth not she.
These babes for Clarence weep and so do I;
I for an Edward weep; so do not they.
Alas, you three, on me, threefold distressed,
Pour all your tears. I am your sorrow’s nurse,
And I will pamper it with lamentations.

units.speaker: DORSET
units.text:
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH] Comfort, dear mother. God is
much displeased
That you take with unthankfulness, his doing.
In common worldly things, ’tis called ungrateful
With dull unwillingness to repay a debt
Which with a bounteous hand was kindly lent;
Much more to be thus opposite with heaven,
For it requires the royal debt it lent you.

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text:
Madam, bethink you, like a careful mother,
Of the young prince your son. Send straight for him.
Let him be crowned. In him your comfort lives.
Drown desperate sorrow in dead Edward’s grave
And plant your joys in living Edward’s throne.
Enter RICHARD, BUCKINGHAM, STANLEY, HASTINGS, and RATCLIFFE

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Sister, have comfort. All of us have cause
To wail the dimming of our shining star,
But none can help our harms by wailing them.—
Madam, my mother, I do cry you mercy;
I did not see your Grace. Humbly on my knee
I crave your blessing.
He kneels

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text:
God bless thee, and put meekness in thy breast,
Love, charity, obedience, and true duty.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
[standing] Amen. [aside] And make me die a good old
man!
That is the butt end of a mother’s blessing;
I marvel that her Grace did leave it out.

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text:
You cloudy princes and heart-sorrowing peers
That bear this heavy mutual load of moan,
Now cheer each other in each other’s love.
Though we have spent our harvest of this king,
We are to reap the harvest of his son.
The broken rancor of your high-swoll'n hates,
But lately splintered, knit, and joined together,
Must gently be preserved, cherished, and kept.
Meseemeth good that with some little train
Forthwith from Ludlow the young prince be fet
Hither to London, to be crowned our king.

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text: Why “with some little train,” my Lord of Buckingham?

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text:
Marry, my lord, lest by a multitude
The new-healed wound of malice should break out,
Which would be so much the more dangerous
By how much the estate is green and yet ungoverned.
Where every horse bears his commanding rein
And may direct his course as please himself,
As well the fear of harm as harm apparent,
In my opinion, ought to be prevented.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
I hope the king made peace with all of us;
And the compact is firm and true in me.

units.speaker: RIVERS
units.text:
And so in me, and so, I think, in all.
Yet since it is but green, it should be put
To no apparent likelihood of breach,
Which haply by much company might be urged.
Therefore I say with noble Buckingham
That it is meet so few should fetch the prince.

units.speaker: HASTINGS
units.text: And so say I.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Then be it so, and go we to determine
Who they shall be that straight shall post to Ludlow.—
Madam, and you, my sister, will you go
To give your censures in this business?
Exeunt all but BUCKINGHAM and RICHARD

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text:
My lord, whoever journeys to the prince,
For God’s sake let not us two at home.
For by the way I’ll sort occasion,
As index to the story we late talked of,
To part the queen’s proud kindred from the prince.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
My other self, my council’s consistory,
My oracle, my prophet, my dear cousin,
I, as a child, will go by thy direction
Toward Ludlow then, for we’ll not stay behind.
Exeunt
Enter one CITIZEN at one door, and another at the other

units.speaker: FIRST CITIZEN
units.text: Good morrow, neighbor, whither away so fast?

units.speaker: SECOND CITIZEN
units.text:
I promise you I scarcely know myself.
Hear you the news abroad?

units.speaker: FIRST CITIZEN
units.text: Yes, that the king is dead.

units.speaker: SECOND CITIZEN
units.text:
Ill news, by 'r Lady. Seldom comes the better.
I fear, I fear, ’twill prove a giddy world.
Enter THIRD CITIZEN

units.speaker: THIRD CITIZEN
units.text: Neighbors, God speed.

units.speaker: FIRST CITIZEN
units.text: Give you good morrow, sir.

units.speaker: THIRD CITIZEN
units.text: Doth this news hold of good King Edward’s death?

units.speaker: SECOND CITIZEN
units.text: Ay, sir, it is too true, God help the while.

units.speaker: THIRD CITIZEN
units.text: Then, masters, look to see a troublous world.

units.speaker: FIRST CITIZEN
units.text: No, no, by God’s good grace, his son shall reign.

units.speaker: THIRD CITIZEN
units.text: Woe to the land that’s governed by a child.

units.speaker: SECOND CITIZEN
units.text:
In him there is a hope of government,
Which, in his nonage, council under him,
And, in his full and ripened years, himself,
No doubt shall then, and till then, govern well.

units.speaker: FIRST CITIZEN
units.text:
So stood the state when Henry the Sixth
Was crowned in Paris but at nine months old.

units.speaker: THIRD CITIZEN
units.text:
Stood the state so? No, no, good friends, God wot,
For then this land was famously enriched
With politic grave counsel; then the king
Had virtuous uncles to protect his Grace.

units.speaker: FIRST CITIZEN
units.text: Why, so hath this, both by the father and mother.

units.speaker: THIRD CITIZEN
units.text:
Better it were they all came by his father,
Or by the father there were none at all,
For emulation who shall now be nearest
Will touch us all too near if God prevent not.
O, full of danger is the duke of Gloucester,
And the queen’s sons and brothers haught and proud,
And were they to be ruled, and not to rule,
This sickly land might solace as before.

units.speaker: FIRST CITIZEN
units.text: Come, come, we fear the worst. All will be well.

units.speaker: THIRD CITIZEN
SECOND CITIZEN
units.text:
Truly, the hearts of men are full of fear.
Ye cannot reason almost with a man
That looks not heavily and full of dread.

units.speaker: THIRD CITIZEN
units.text:
Before the days of change, still is it so.
By a divine instinct, men’s minds mistrust
Ensuing dangers, as by proof we see
The water swell before a boist'rous storm.
But leave it all to God. Whither away?

units.speaker: SECOND CITIZEN
units.text: Marry, we were sent for to the justices.

units.speaker: THIRD CITIZEN
units.text: And so was I. I’ll bear you company.
Exeunt
Enter the ARCHBISHOP of York, the young duke of YORK, QUEEN ELIZABETH, and the old DUCHESS of York

units.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
units.text:
Last night, I hear, they lay at Stony Stratford,
And at Northampton they do rest tonight.
Tomorrow or next day they will be here.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text:
I long with all my heart to see the prince.
I hope he is much grown since last I saw him.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
But I hear no; they say my son of York
Has almost overta'en him in his growth.

units.speaker: YORK
units.text: Ay, mother, but I would not have it so.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: Why, my young cousin? It is good to grow.

units.speaker: YORK
units.text:
Grandam, one night as we did sit at supper,
My uncle Rivers talked how I did grow
More than my brother: “Ay,” quoth my uncle Gloucester,
“Small herbs have grace; great weeds do grow apace.”
And since, methinks I would not grow so fast
Because sweet flowers are slow and weeds make haste.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text:
Good faith, good faith, the saying did not hold
In him that did object the same to thee!
He was the wretched’st thing when he was young,
So long a-growing and so leisurely,
That if this rule were true, he should be gracious.

units.speaker: YORK
units.text: And so no doubt he is, my gracious madam.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: I hope he is, but yet let mothers doubt.

units.speaker: YORK
units.text:
Now, by my troth, if I had been remembered,
I could have given my uncle’s grace a flout
To touch his growth nearer than he touched mine.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: How, my pretty York? I prithee let me hear it.

units.speaker: YORK
units.text:
Marry, they say my uncle grew so fast
That he could gnaw a crust at two hours old.
'Twas full two years ere I could get a tooth.
Grandam, this would have been a biting jest.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: I prithee, pretty York, who told thee this?

units.speaker: YORK
units.text: Grandam, his nurse.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: His nurse? Why, she was dead ere thou wast born.

units.speaker: YORK
units.text: If ’twere not she, I cannot tell who told me.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: A parlous boy! Go to, you are too shrewd.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: Good madam, be not angry with the child.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: Pitchers have ears.
Enter a MESSENGER

units.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
units.text: Here comes a messenger. —What news?

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: Such news, my lord, as grieves me to report.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: How doth the prince?

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: Well, madam, and in health.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: What is thy news then?

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text:
Lord Rivers and Lord Grey are sent to Pomfret,
And, with them, Sir Thomas Vaughan, prisoners.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: Who hath committed them?

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text: The mighty dukes, Gloucester and Buckingham.

units.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
units.text: For what offence?

units.speaker: MESSENGER
units.text:
The sum of all I can, I have disclosed.
Why, or for what, the nobles were committed
Is all unknown to me, my gracious lord.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
Ay me! I see the ruin of my house.
The tiger now hath seized the gentle hind.
Insulting tyranny begins to jut
Upon the innocent and aweless throne.
Welcome, destruction, blood, and massacre.
I see, as in a map, the end of all.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text:
Accursèd and unquiet wrangling days,
How many of you have mine eyes beheld?
My husband lost his life to get the crown,
And often up and down my sons were tossed
For me to joy, and weep, their gain and loss.
And being seated, and domestic broils
Clean overblown, themselves the conquerors
Make war upon themselves, brother to brother,
Blood to blood, self against self. O, preposterous
And frantic outrage, end thy damnèd spleen,
Or let me die, to look on death no more.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text:
[to YORK] Come, come, my boy. We will to sanctuary.
Madam, farewell.

units.speaker: DUCHESS
units.text: Stay, I will go with you.

units.speaker: QUEEN ELIZABETH
units.text: You have no cause.

units.speaker: ARCHBISHOP
units.text:
[to QUEEN ELIZABETH]
My gracious lady, go,
And thither bear your treasure and your goods.
For my part, I’ll resign unto your Grace
The seal I keep; and so betide to me
As well I tender you and all of yours.
Go. I’ll conduct you to the sanctuary.
Exeunt
The trumpets sound. Enter the young PRINCE, the CARDINAL, CATESBY, and others

units.speaker: BUCKINGHAM
units.text: Welcome, sweet prince, to London, to your chamber.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
[to PRINCE] Welcome, dear cousin, my thoughts'
sovereign.
The weary way hath made you melancholy.

units.speaker: PRINCE
units.text:
No, uncle, but our crosses on the way
Have made it tedious, wearisome, and heavy.
I want more uncles here to welcome me.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text:
Sweet prince, the untainted virtue of your years
Hath not yet dived into the world’s deceit;
Nor more can you distinguish of a man
Than of his outward show, which, God He knows,
Seldom or never jumpeth with the heart.
Those uncles which you want were dangerous.
Your Grace attended to their sugared words
But looked not on the poison of their hearts.
God keep you from them, and from such false friends.

units.speaker: PRINCE
units.text: God keep me from false friends, but they were none.

units.speaker: RICHARD
units.text: My lord, the mayor of London comes to greet you.
Enter the LORD MAYOR and his train

units.speaker: LORD MAYOR
units.text: God bless your Grace with health and happy days.

units.speaker: PRINCE
units.text:
